cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning on the Los Gatos Creek Trail with a easy walk along the creek, enjoy the cool air and the shade before the day warms up.
cards.why: Perfect morning temperature for a walk before the crowds show up.

cards.name: Pure Massage Therapy
cards.address: 116 E Campbell Ave Suite 7, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Pure Massage Therapy on Campbell Ave and actually decompress for an hour.
cards.why: A weekday morning slot here is the move when it's not packed.

cards.name: Hinodeya Campbell Town
cards.address: 2210 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Hinodeya Campbell Town for a bowl of ramen, rich broth and house noodles done the right way.
cards.why: Solid ramen in Campbell that holds up every single time.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the galleries at Triton Museum of Art and spend some time in the sculpture garden out back.
cards.why: Free contemporary art and no crowds on a Thursday afternoon.

cards.name: Trombone Studio Recital at Music Building
cards.blurb: Swing by the Music Building at SJSU for the Trombone Studio Recital, a free live performance worth catching.
cards.why: Live music for free on a weekday is a genuinely good use of an hour.

cards.name: The Bywater
cards.address: 532 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at The Bywater in Los Gatos, a solid dinner spot with good food and a relaxed vibe.
cards.why: It's the kind of dinner that actually feels like a proper end to a good day.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning on the Los Gatos Creek Trail with the kids. Easy, flat, and shaded along the creek — great for little legs on bikes or on foot.
cards.why: 55 degrees and crisp is perfect trail weather before it warms up.

cards.name: The Good Salad
cards.address: 397 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by The Good Salad on Campbell Ave for a fresh mid-morning bite. Great spot to fuel up the family before heading out for the afternoon.
cards.why: One of the best-rated spots in Campbell and it opens right at 10.

cards.name: The Tech Interactive
cards.address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: The Tech Interactive in downtown San Jose is basically a giant playground disguised as a museum. Kids can build, hack, design, and catch an IMAX show while they're at it.
cards.why: Nothing burns a Thursday afternoon better for kids than this place.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Stop at San Pedro Square Market for lunch from whatever looks good. Tacos, banh mi, ramen, coffee — everyone picks their own thing and you grab a patio table.
cards.why: It's a no-argument lunch spot when the family can't agree on one thing.

cards.name: Homework Help K-6 grade
cards.blurb: Homework Help for K-6 at the Saratoga library is a solid way to knock out any school assignments before the weekend. Drop-in style, free, and the kids leave with less Thursday-night stress.
cards.why: Getting homework done before Friday is a win for everyone.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Vasona Lake County Park for a relaxed late afternoon walk. Kids love the little train, there's a lake to look at, and it's a nice way to wind down before dinner.
cards.why: The high hits 69 today, so the late afternoon at the lake will feel great.

cards.name: Baskin-Robbins
cards.address: 15730 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the day with a scoop at Baskin-Robbins on Los Gatos Blvd. Classic end to a family day out.
cards.why: Ice cream is always the right call after a full day with kids.

cards.name: Puffin Donuts
cards.address: 1442 Pollard Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a donut (or two) from Puffin — the ratings speak for themselves and the vibe is low-key perfect for easing into a Thursday.
cards.why: Best way to kick off a morning in Los Gatos.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail and shake off the morning fog. It's shaded, flat, and follows the creek the whole way — great for a casual stroll or picking up the pace a bit.
cards.why: 55°F with a high of 68 makes this the ideal window for a creek walk.

cards.name: Drop-in Tech Help
cards.blurb: Swing by the Saratoga Library for Drop-in Tech Help. Bring any device questions you've been putting off and get actual answers from someone who knows what they're doing.
cards.why: Free, no appointment needed, and genuinely useful.

cards.name: TaoTaoTea
cards.address: 18472 Prospect Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch tea and a bite at TaoTaoTea on Prospect Rd. The menu is light and the space is cozy, good for sitting down after a busy morning.
cards.why: Closes at 4 PM so this is the sweet spot to catch it.

cards.name: Teatime Book Chatter
cards.blurb: Head to Teatime Book Chatter at the Saratoga Library. It's a casual drop-in conversation about books over tea. No prep required, just show up and chat.
cards.why: A genuinely fun afternoon thing that most people would never think to try on a Thursday.

cards.name: Cloud 9 Foot Spa San Jose - Formerly Oceanic
cards.address: 2989 Union Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Book a foot massage at Cloud 9 Foot Spa and just decompress for an hour. It's a solid mid-afternoon reset before heading into the evening.
cards.why: You've earned it after a full morning and afternoon.

cards.name: Learn to Play Mahjong
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Learn to Play Mahjong at 110 E Main St in Los Gatos. Whether you're a total beginner or vaguely familiar, it's a fun and social way to spend an early evening.
cards.why: Free, social, and something genuinely different for a Thursday night.

cards.name: One Fish Raw Bar
cards.address: 193 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at One Fish Raw Bar in Campbell. Order something from the raw bar, split a few plates, and take your time with it.
cards.why: It's the kind of dinner spot that makes a weeknight feel like a weekend.

cards.name: Los Gatos Cafe Downtown
cards.address: 340 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at Los Gatos Cafe Downtown. Grab breakfast and coffee while the kids settle in — it's a cozy spot on Santa Cruz Ave that feels like a proper start to a day out.
cards.why: Good food, casual vibe, and the perfect way to ease into the day.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Vasona Lake County Park and let the kids run loose. Walk the easy trail around the lake, watch the ducks, and if the little train is running, hop on for a ride around the park.
cards.why: Kids absolutely love this place and the lake views make it worth it for adults too.

cards.name: Teatime Book Chatter
cards.blurb: Swing by Saratoga Library for Teatime Book Chatter. It's a casual, drop-in style book discussion that's free and a nice low-key afternoon activity.
cards.why: Free, fun, and a great way to sneak in some reading time with the kids.

cards.name: Homework Help K-6 grade
cards.blurb: Hit up the Homework Help session at the Saratoga Library for K-6 kids. Volunteers are on hand to help work through anything the kids have due this week.
cards.why: Getting homework done early means the rest of the evening is all yours.

cards.name: Baskin-Robbins
cards.address: 15730 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the afternoon with a scoop (or two) at Baskin-Robbins on Los Gatos Blvd. Classic flavors, kid-approved, and a perfect end-of-day treat.
cards.why: Ice cream after homework? That's a deal every kid will remember.

cards.name: How Exploration, Uncertainty, and Risk Help Us Find Meaning with Author Alex Hutchinson
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Author Alex Hutchinson is talking about how exploration, uncertainty, and risk shape a meaningful life. Good conversation for a Thursday morning if you're into that kind of thing.
cards.why: Free talk, interesting subject, and it fits perfectly after the museum.

cards.name: Fish With You鱼你在一起Mountain View
cards.address: 156 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Fish With You on Castro and order one of their malatang or fish hot pot bowls. Bold flavors, solid lunch spot.
cards.why: Castro Street is the move for lunch and this place delivers.

cards.name: Electrify Your Home: A Community Workshop
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Drop into this free community workshop on electrifying your home. Practical, relevant, and you'll probably leave knowing something you didn't before.
cards.why: Free evening event and actually useful if you own or rent in the area.

cards.name: Next Level Board Game Cafe
cards.address: 888 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Next Level Board Game Cafe has hundreds of games to pick from. Grab a drink, pull something off the shelf, and settle in for a couple hours.
cards.why: Best way to spend a Thursday afternoon with no agenda.

cards.name: 1 Oz Coffee
cards.address: 650 Castro St #130, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a solid coffee and a pastry on Castro Street before the crowds show up.
cards.why: Good coffee makes everything easier, especially with kids in tow.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the morning at the Computer History Museum — there are hands-on exhibits, giant old computers, and enough wow-moments to keep kids genuinely curious the whole time.
cards.why: It's way more fun than it sounds, and the kids will actually talk about it later.

cards.name: The Future of Humanoid Robots
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Catch The Future of Humanoid Robots exhibition while you're already at the museum. Robots walking around and doing things in real life is the kind of stuff kids absolutely lose their minds over.
cards.why: You're already there, and this one is a genuine crowd-pleaser for the younger set.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head over to Shoreline Park and let the kids run around the open fields, walk along the water, or watch the birds do their thing out on the Bay.
cards.why: The high will hit 69°F by afternoon, which is exactly the right temperature to be outside.

cards.name: Mia's Paleteria Y Neveria
cards.address: 120 N Rengstorff Ave Suite A, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Mia's Paleteria for paletas and nieves. Let the kids pick their flavors and eat them outside.
cards.why: Afternoon paletas after a park walk is basically a perfect move.

cards.name: Cheeky Monkey Toys
cards.address: 173 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Cheeky Monkey Toys in Los Altos, a fantastic independent toy shop where kids can explore and you might actually find something worth buying.
cards.why: Way more interesting than a big box store, and the selection is genuinely thoughtful.

cards.name: Bavetta Coffee
cards.address: 754 The Alameda #80, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at Bavetta Coffee on The Alameda. Grab a coffee and settle in before the day gets going.
cards.why: Good coffee and a chill vibe to ease into a Thursday.

cards.name: Natalie and James Thompson Gallery: Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at ART 127: Thompson Gallery
cards.blurb: Head to the Thompson Gallery for Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology. It's a free art show worth wandering through slowly.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a great way to spend a weekday morning.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch. Mix and match from a bunch of vendors — tacos, banh mi, ramen — and grab a patio seat if the sun's out.
cards.why: Easiest call for lunch downtown, no debate needed.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk it off along Guadalupe River Park. It's greener than you'd expect for the middle of downtown and a good way to decompress after eating.
cards.why: Perfect afternoon weather for a low-key stroll.

cards.name: Art and Craft Workshops
cards.address: 255 N Market St #270, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Art and Craft Workshops on N Market St. Great spot to make something with your hands for a couple hours.
cards.why: A surprisingly fun way to spend a weekday afternoon if you've never tried it.

cards.name: Sarika Thai Massage & Spa
cards.address: 1375 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Sarika Thai Massage and Spa on Lincoln Ave. Let the day wind down properly before dinner.
cards.why: Hard to beat a massage as the afternoon transition into evening.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the night at Santana Row. Grab dinner at one of the spots along the boulevard, then stroll the shops if you're not ready to head home.
cards.why: The evening atmosphere here is genuinely nice and worth lingering in.

cards.name: Voyager Craft Coffee
cards.address: 3985 Stevens Creek Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Kick the morning off with coffee and a pastry here before the day gets going. Good vibes, easy pace, and the kids can settle in before the action starts.
cards.why: Every good day starts with decent coffee and this place delivers.

cards.name: Santa Clara University Women’s Tennis vs Washington State
cards.blurb: Catch the SCU Women's Tennis team take on Washington State. Free to watch, easy to follow, and kids actually enjoy the back-and-forth of live tennis.
cards.why: Free live sports on a Thursday is a genuinely good deal.

cards.name: Elyse Restaurant
cards.address: 96 S 1st St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the day with dinner at Elyse. Good food, relaxed setting, a solid way to close out a full day with the family.
cards.why: You've earned a proper sit-down dinner after a packed day.

cards.name: ZombieRunner Coffee
cards.address: 344 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at ZombieRunner Coffee on California Ave — grab a coffee and settle in for a bit before the day picks up.
cards.why: It's a great local spot to ease into a Thursday morning.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Head up The Dish Trail for a solid morning hike through the Stanford foothills. The views open up big as you climb and the 56°F start means you'll warm up perfectly on the trail.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a hike that actually earns its views.

cards.name: Seasons Noodles & Dumplings Garden
cards.address: 702 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Post-hike lunch at Seasons Noodles and Dumplings Garden. Get a bowl of noodles and a plate of dumplings and let your legs recover.
cards.why: Hot noodles after a morning hike is exactly what you want.

cards.name: Sustainability Data Science Conference
cards.address: 389 Jane Stanford Way, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Drop into the Sustainability Data Science Conference at 389 Jane Stanford Way. It's open and free, so poke around whatever sessions catch your eye.
cards.why: Free access to genuinely interesting talks happening today on campus.
cards.venue: Simonyi Conference Center, CoDa, 389 Jane Stanford Way, Stanford, CA 94305

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk over to Cantor Arts Center and spend some time with the permanent collection. Don't skip the Rodin Sculpture Garden out front.
cards.why: Free world-class art and you're already on campus.

cards.name: Color Me Mine
cards.address: 321 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Color Me Mine on University Ave and paint something. Walk-ins are welcome and you pick whatever piece you want to paint.
cards.why: A low-key, genuinely fun way to spend an early evening.

cards.name: Stylish Sustainability Craft
cards.blurb: Wrap up at Color Me Mine and head to the Stylish Sustainability Craft event in Los Altos at 6:30. It's a free hands-on craft night with a sustainability angle.
cards.why: A free evening event that keeps the creative vibe going.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Give the kids a solid two hours to geek out on vintage computers, the history of the internet, and hands-on exhibits that actually hold their attention.
cards.why: The Computer History Museum is genuinely wild for kids who've never seen where all this tech came from.

cards.name: Driftwood Deli & Market
cards.address: 3450 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Driftwood for sandwiches and snacks to refuel after the museum.
cards.why: Casual, affordable, and exactly what you need mid-afternoon with hungry kids.

cards.name: Cornelis Bol Park
cards.address: 3590 Laguna Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Let the kids run loose at Cornelis Bol Park while the afternoon warms up to its peak.
cards.why: A beautiful little park that gives kids room to move after a morning of sitting and looking.

cards.name: Stylish Sustainability Craft
cards.blurb: Get crafty with the family at this free sustainability-themed craft night in Los Altos.
cards.why: Free, creative, and something the kids can actually make and take home.
